item.title: Tarrifs are paid by U.S. consumers.
item.description: For the last time, for the people in the back, we, the United States citizens, are paying for every dime in tariffs.

I do reviews as a hobby, and this week I made a review of "Neverending Story" Yes, the classic. If you feel up to see it, check here! https://youtu.be/qAt8ZX6nOFM or if you're in a reading mood, https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/

item.title: AI Art Dystopia
item.description: There’s so much wrong with this. The AI being used to find AI art, the mods insta-banning with no chance for fixing if there’s a mistake, blaming the artist for making art that got flagged without checking it wouldn’t, the mod’s attitude towards the artist, the unwillingness to work with the artist, the mod’s arrogance that the artist would want to come back after being treated like crap…
item.dc:creator: jerseyPizza

item.title: When you let democracy spoil, you get MAGAts.
item.description:
Yes.
They will have to admit they’re wrong eventually. But get them away from the influence before deprogramming them.

item.title: Are these foundational truths in the room with us now?
item.description: He knows what he is doing, and it will work on the set of people it needs to work on. Same reason why they have a banner on .gov sites informing people that democrats are responsible for the shut down, no serious person believes that statement but a set of people will. For the record, I’m ok with aligning with most of the groups he lists out. Any group opposed to the Republican Party has common ground with me,
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Perfect Blue Mima cosplay by YuzuPyon
item.description: Extended version here : https://youtu.be/-ACFFx9Ruqo I got last minute tickets to go watch one of my favorite movie of all time so I decided to speedrun putting this costume together in 4 hours! :) it was fun to vlog, the full video of the process and theater footage in on my Youtube channel! :) Perfect Blue is my favorite psychological horror movie of all time. It influenced many movies and directors afterwards, including western movies like Requiem for a Dream and Black Swan that have drawn lots of scenes and inspiration from it. It may seem like a paradox but the older you get the more intense a rewatch becomes in my opinion, at least that's how it felt for me. In my opinion, this movie is perfect in every way. From the way how real and unsettling its many topics are depicted, to the animation quality, to the general direction, this movie is truly a timeless masterpiece. I was really happy and honored people wanted to take pictures with me at the movies, but also seeing so much enthusiasm for the movie made me really emotional. People clapped at the end of the movie, which I never seen happen in theaters before! Have a good day, Yuzu
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: Wish me luck friends. And Jen.
item.description: I have an interview tomorrow with ‘Book with Jacob’ a scam artist with a decent set up, I’m recording the whole thing to make a pop-up video style edit for my son to help him spot fake interviews and hopefully realize how theatrical the internet can be. Edit: I will make sure to upload snippets and a link to what I make and follow ups. I’ll tag with “Fartface” if you want to look for it.
item.dc:creator: TranscendentMonkeyNutsRupturingAndReIeasingAVelociraptor

item.title: OG Meme Dump 251015
item.dc:creator: lem888

item.title: Pentagon journalists exit en masse over new reporting restrictions
item.description:
Dozens of reporters have turned in their access badges and exited the Pentagon rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work. Several global media organisations have refused to sign a pledge to refrain from reporting information not authorised for release by the Pentagon.
TRUMP said "The press is very dishonest."
News outlets were unanimous in rejecting the rules imposed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. The order would leave journalists vulnerable to expulsion if they sought to report on information – classified or otherwise – that had not been approved by Mr Hegseth for release.
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: Dexter, the young tiger from Kharkiv Ecopark, is growing up in the blink of an eye. Just look at that furry loaf passed out on his human like it’s a comfy mattress.

item.title: Ohio Congressman’s office displays Nazi symbol
item.description: Greater Cincinnati's newest congressman condemned a "vile" symbol at his Washington, D.C. office. Rep. Dave Taylor, a Republican whose district includes Clermont and other southern Ohio counties, reacted in an emailed statement on Wednesday, Oct. 15 to an "inappropriate" symbol found in his office. "I am aware of an image that appears to depict a vile and deeply inappropriate symbol near an employee in my office," the statement read. "The content of that image does not reflect the values or standards of this office, my staff, or myself, and I condemn it in the strongest terms. Upon learning of this matter, I immediately directed a thorough investigation alongside Capitol Police, which remains ongoing. No further comment will be provided until it has been completed." The press release from Taylor's office said the congressman was responding to "vandalism" at his office. The release did not describe what the symbol looked like, and his office did not provide a photo. Around 10 minutes after Taylor's office emailed the release, the left-leaning Ohio politics blogger D.J. Byrnes posted a photo on X. The photo showed a screenshot from a video call with one of Taylor's staffers, with a swastika American flag in the background. The person at the desk was Taylor's legislative correspondent, according to the post. The staffer, Angelo Elia, is listed as Taylor's legislative correspondent on LegiStorm, a website that publishes congressional data and contact information. The Enquirer emailed Taylor's spokesperson twice asking whether the purported photo of the office is connected to Taylor's statement about the "vandalism." Who is Rep. Dave Taylor? Taylor won a crowded Republican primary last year and went on to succeed former Rep. Brad Wenstrup to represent Ohio's 2nd Congressional District. He began his first term in January. https://www.cincinnati.com/story/news/2025/10/15/dave-taylor-reacts-vile-symbol-office/86709735007/
item.dc:creator: lurkyloos
